name: Sunflower Theorem

phase_0.boundary_condition: The closed spiral of seed positions that returns to itself at infinity, bounded by the Fibonacci limit

VINIT.justification: Before any spiral pattern exists, the flower head is void — no angular positions assigned, no Fibonacci sequence begun

TANCH.chosen_element: the complete spiral pattern at convergence
TANCH.justification: The terminal anchor that contains all seed positions; the closed boundary where the phyllotactic spiral reaches its limit and self-references

AFWD.justification: Forward morphism: each new seed advances the spiral outward and rotates by the golden angle, driving growth toward the limit

AREV.justification: Reverse morphism: unwinding the spiral backward, descending from outer seeds to inner seeds, undoing the angular accumulation

CLINK.justification: Sequential chaining: each seed position is the composition of all prior golden-angle rotations; n-th seed = n × 137.5° mod 360°

IMSCRIB.justification: Identity: the spiral pattern is self-similar at every scale; the Fibonacci sequence embedded in seed counts recognizes its own recursive structure

FSPLIT.chosen_element: bifurcation into clockwise and counterclockwise spirals
FSPLIT.justification: One spiral branches into two observable spiral arms (one clockwise, one counterclockwise) visible in sunflower seed arrangement; both emerge from the same angular rule

FFUSE.chosen_element: reconstitution of unified spiral from dual arms
FFUSE.justification: The two spiral arms (clockwise and counterclockwise) fuse back into the single underlying golden-angle rule; FFUSE(FSPLIT(spiral)) = spiral

EVALT.justification: Affirmative: the ratio of consecutive Fibonacci numbers approaches φ = (1+√5)/2; the spiral is mathematically valid and self-consistent

EVALF.chosen_element: spiral diverges or fails to close
EVALF.justification: Negative: if the angle deviates from 137.5°, the spiral does not pack efficiently; seeds overlap or leave gaps; the pattern breaks

ENGAGR.justification: Paradice: both spiral directions are present and equally valid; the observer holds both orientations without choosing one, a bicameral compromise in perception

IFIX.chosen_element: each seed position recorded as permanent
IFIX.justification: ROM fixation: once a seed is placed at position n × 137.5°, that position is immutable; the spiral grows append-only, never revising prior seeds

phase_2.split_element: bifurcation into clockwise and counterclockwise spiral arms
split_outputs: clockwise Fibonacci spiral (consecutive Fibonacci numbers as radii)
counterclockwise Fibonacci spiral (same Fibonacci numbers, opposite rotation)
phase_2.fuse_element: reconstitution of the single underlying angular rule
phase_2.fuse_result: the unified golden-angle spiral (137.5° per step, Fibonacci radii) — identical to split_input

phase_3.void_description: 00 — no spiral exists; seed disk is blank; no angular positions assigned; Fibonacci sequence not yet instantiated
phase_3.true_description: 01 — spiral converges to golden ratio; seeds pack efficiently; consecutive Fibonacci ratios approach φ; pattern is self-consistent and observable
phase_3.false_description: 10 — spiral diverges; seeds overlap or scatter; angle deviates from 137.5°; Fibonacci ratio fails to converge; pattern is broken or invalid
phase_3.both_description: 11 — paradice: both clockwise and counterclockwise spirals are simultaneously present and equally valid; observer holds both without resolution; bicameral spiral state

steps.domain_action: uninitialized seed disk — no positions, no angles, no Fibonacci sequence
the complete spiral pattern at convergence — boundary that contains all seeds and closes the system
first seed placed at 0° — forward morphism begins; angular increment rule established
composition of first angular step — seed 1 at 137.5° (1 × golden angle)
second seed placed at 275° — forward morphism continues; spiral grows outward
composition of second angular step — seed 2 at 2 × 137.5° = 275° mod 360°
third seed placed at 52.5° — forward morphism continues; spiral wraps around
composition of third angular step — seed 3 at 3 × 137.5° = 412.5° mod 360° = 52.5°
fourth seed placed at 190° — forward morphism continues; spiral densifies
composition of fourth angular step — seed 4 at 4 × 137.5° = 550° mod 360° = 190°
fifth seed placed at 327.5° — forward morphism continues; Fibonacci number 5 reached
composition of fifth angular step — seed 5 at 5 × 137.5° = 687.5° mod 360° = 327.5°
bifurcation into clockwise and counterclockwise spiral arms — one spiral branches into two observable directions
clockwise arm converges — consecutive Fibonacci numbers form radii; ratio approaches φ
clockwise spiral continues outward — seeds 6, 7, 8... placed at golden angles, clockwise direction
composition of clockwise steps — each seed position is sum of all prior rotations
counterclockwise arm converges — same Fibonacci radii, opposite rotation direction
counterclockwise spiral traced backward — reverse morphism unwinding the clockwise sequence
composition of counterclockwise steps — each seed position is sum of all prior reverse rotations
both spiral arms held simultaneously — observer perceives clockwise and counterclockwise without choosing; paradice state
spiral recognizes itself — Fibonacci sequence embedded in seed counts; self-similar at every scale; identity of the pattern
reconstitution of unified spiral from dual arms — clockwise and counterclockwise fuse back into single golden-angle rule
unified spiral converges to golden ratio — FFUSE result equals FSPLIT input; pattern is valid and self-consistent
seed position 1 recorded as permanent — 0° position immutable; append-only growth begins
seed position 2 recorded as permanent — 137.5° position immutable; spiral grows irreversibly
seed position 3 recorded as permanent — 275° position immutable; no revision possible
seed position 4 recorded as permanent — 52.5° position immutable; ROM fixation continues
seed position 5 recorded as permanent — 190° position immutable; Fibonacci limit approached
seed position 6 recorded as permanent — 327.5° position immutable; spiral densifies irreversibly
seed position 7 recorded as permanent — 104.5° position immutable; pattern crystallizes
seed position 8 recorded as permanent — 242° position immutable; Fibonacci 8 reached
seed position 13 recorded as permanent — Fibonacci 13 reached; spiral converges further
seed position 21 recorded as permanent — Fibonacci 21 reached; golden ratio ratio = 21/13 ≈ 1.615
seed position 34 recorded as permanent — Fibonacci 34 reached; golden ratio ratio = 34/21 ≈ 1.619
seed position 55 recorded as permanent — Fibonacci 55 reached; golden ratio ratio = 55/34 ≈ 1.6176
composition of all angular steps — the entire spiral is the chaining of n × 137.5° for all n
the spiral recognizes its own recursive structure — Fibonacci sequence self-references; pattern is identity
the complete spiral pattern at convergence — boundary closes; all seeds positioned; system is complete
final self-reference — the spiral returns to itself; the pattern recognizes its own closure

phase_5.compiler_frontend: The golden-angle rule (137.5° = 360° × (1 - 1/φ)) — translates the Fibonacci sequence into angular positions; compiles abstract ratio into concrete seed placement
phase_5.ipc_mechanism: Angular increment protocol — each seed communicates its position to the next via the golden angle; seeds coordinate through rotational offset, not direct messaging
phase_5.memory_mechanism: Fibonacci sequence stored in seed count; each seed's radial distance encodes its Fibonacci index; spiral geometry is the memory structure itself
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: Temporal growth order — seeds are placed sequentially from center outward; time is encoded in radial distance; older seeds are inner, newer seeds are outer
phase_5.alfs_store: The golden ratio φ = (1+√5)/2 — the canonical reference; all seed positions derive from this single constant; φ is the bootstrap store

phase_6.cycle_cost: Each seed placement consumes one angular increment (137.5°) and one Fibonacci step; growth is irreversible (IFIX); no energy is recovered
phase_6.pre_cycle_state: Spiral at generation n — n seeds placed, n Fibonacci numbers used, n × 137.5° total rotation accumulated
phase_6.post_cycle_state: Spiral at generation n+1 — n+1 seeds placed, n+1 Fibonacci numbers used, (n+1) × 137.5° total rotation accumulated
phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — the spiral is a closed system; the golden-angle rule is deterministic and reversible in principle (AREV can unwind AFWD); the Frobenius constraint FFUSE(FSPLIT(spiral)) = spiral ensures no information is lost; growth is append-only but the underlying pattern is entropy-conservative

lean_scaffold:
-- IGProtocol scaffold: VINIT → TANCH → AFWD → CLINK → AFWD → CLINK → AFWD → CLINK → AFWD → CLINK → AFWD → CLINK → FSPLIT → EVALT → AFWD → CLINK → EVALF → AREV → CLINK → ENGAGR → IMSCRIB → FFUSE → EVALT → IFIX → IFIX → IFIX → IFIX → IFIX → IFIX → IFIX → IFIX → IFIX → IFIX → IFIX → IFIX → CLINK → IMSCRIB → TANCH → IMSCRIB
-- Class: Sunflower Theorem
-- Fingerprint: sig=(21,2,4,12)
--   self_ref=False | frobenius_order=1
--   dialetheia_complete=True | period=39
-- Expected tier: O₂
-- FSPLIT/FFUSE pairs: [(12, 21)]

import Imscribing.IGFunctor

namespace Imscribing

open Dimensionality Topology Relational Polarity Grammar

  (.arrow 𐑾 𐑱 𐑱)  -- [8] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (fourth seed placed at 190° — forward morphism continues; spiral densifies)
  (.arrow 𐑱 𐑾 𐑾)  -- [9] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (composition of fourth angular step — seed 4 at 4 × 137.5° = 550° mod 360° = 190°)
  (.arrow 𐑾 𐑱 𐑱)  -- [10] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (fifth seed placed at 327.5° — forward morphism continues; Fibonacci number 5 ...)
  (.arrow 𐑱 𐑾 𐑚)  -- [11] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (composition of fifth angular step — seed 5 at 5 × 137.5° = 687.5° mod 360° = ...)
  -- FSPLIT [12] (gran := 𐑚) (bifurcation into clockwise and counterclockwise spiral arms — one spiral branches into two observable directions) / FFUSE [21] (stoi := 𐑙)

    (.prod
      -- T-branch (3 nodes)

      .seq

        (.arrow 𐑱 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [15] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (composition of clockwise steps — each seed position is sum of all prior rotat...)
      -- F-branch (5 nodes)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑖 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [16] EVALF | chir := 𐑖 | evaluate-false — chirality check (counterclockwise arm converges — same Fibonacci radii, opposite rotation dire...)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑗 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [17] AREV | pol := 𐑗 | reverse morphism — parity flip (counterclockwise spiral traced backward — reverse morphism unwinding the cloc...)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑱 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [18] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (composition of counterclockwise steps — each seed position is sum of all prio...)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑳 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [19] ENGAGR | stoi := 𐑳 | engage paradox — B-state, both arms (both spiral arms held simultaneously — observer perceives clockwise and count...)
        (.arrow 𐑠 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [20] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | identity — self-imscription (spiral recognizes itself — Fibonacci sequence embedded in seed counts; self-s...))
    -- reconnect at FFUSE [21]: μ closes the Frobenius pair
    (.arrow 𐑙 𐑙 ⊙)  -- [21] FFUSE | stoi := 𐑙 (reconstitution of unified spiral from dual arms — clockwise and counterclockwise fuse back into single golden-angle rule)
  (.arrow ⊙ 𐑙 𐑭)  -- [22] EVALT | crit := ⊙ | evaluate-true — criticality gate open (unified spiral converges to golden ratio — FFUSE result equals FSPLIT input; ...)
  (.arrow 𐑭 ⊙ 𐑭)  -- [23] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | irreversible fixation — winding number (seed position 1 recorded as permanent — 0° position immutable; append-only gr...)
  (.arrow 𐑭 𐑭 𐑭)  -- [24] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | irreversible fixation — winding number (seed position 2 recorded as permanent — 137.5° position immutable; spiral gro...)

  (.arrow 𐑭 𐑭 𐑭)  -- [32] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | irreversible fixation — winding number (seed position 21 recorded as permanent — Fibonacci 21 reached; golden ratio r...)
  (.arrow 𐑭 𐑭 𐑭)  -- [33] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | irreversible fixation — winding number (seed position 34 recorded as permanent — Fibonacci 34 reached; golden ratio r...)
  (.arrow 𐑭 𐑭 𐑱)  -- [34] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | irreversible fixation — winding number (seed position 55 recorded as permanent — Fibonacci 55 reached; golden ratio r...)
  (.arrow 𐑱 𐑭 𐑠)  -- [35] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (composition of all angular steps — the entire spiral is the chaining of n × 1...)
  (.arrow 𐑠 𐑱 𐑡)  -- [36] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | identity — self-imscription (the spiral recognizes its own recursive structure — Fibonacci sequence self-r...)
  (.arrow 𐑡 𐑠 𐑠)  -- [37] TANCH | top := 𐑡 | terminal object — connectivity boundary (the complete spiral pattern at convergence — boundary closes; all seeds posit...)
  (.arrow 𐑠 𐑡 𐑼)  -- [38] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | identity — self-imscription (final self-reference — the spiral returns to itself; the pattern recognizes i...)

-- ── Evaluation arm sub-defs ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

-- truth arm
noncomputable def sunflower_theorem_true_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑠 :=
  (sunflower_theorem_protocol).restrictToEVALT

-- false arm
noncomputable def sunflower_theorem_false_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑠 :=
  (sunflower_theorem_protocol).restrictToEVALF

-- ── Verification theorems ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

theorem sunflower_theorem_tier : TierFunctor.obj 𐑼 = .O₂ := by decide

-- Frobenius (split → fuse): μ∘δ = id on .prod branch
-- Proof: apply igFrobAlg_self_fusion; exact mu_delta_A_id
-- (requires mu_delta_A_id from IGFunctor library)
